mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and a stable or declining federal funds rate creates an optimal growth environment, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising GDPC1 and falling FEDFUNDS fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio on 20-day forward returns, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and short-term S&P 500 upside.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in >60% of months following CPI spikes above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates robust consumer spending capacity and labor market health, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS yields a negative or zero alpha over 20-day horizons, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, serving as a leading indicator of economic expansion that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST growth consistently fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns, or the correlation reverses during periods of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial output and rising unemployment signals a supply-side contraction and weakening demand, forcing equity markets to price in recessionary losses over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration of INDPRO and UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the signal shows no predictive power during late-cycle phases.
